textDisplay:
Sooo, this might just be me and my crazy feminist ideas, but am I the only one who sees the connection between the most recent Purge movie (The Purge: Election Year) and the modern feminist movement that we're living in?
This movie has been advertised for like two(?) years now and I just think it's weird how in this most recent era of woman politicians and feminists saying "hey, don't rape people" among other things like more woman main characters in blockbuster dude dominated roles and women having a louder voice than ever,
this slasher movie comes out where the entire plot is centered around a counsel of dudes who's goal it is to murder the woman politician who's main platform is "hey, don't kill people".... 
It reminds me of what was happening in 70s slasher films with the spawn of the "final girl" trope and the pattern of brutally murdering half naked women in the middle of another big feminist movement.
It just feels like history is repeating it's self here you know? Because as much as people will say "it's just a movie" movies, especially horror/slasher movies are a reflection of the time they're made. And right now we're living in a time of a modern feminist movement and also an antifeminist movement in the most recognizable form of "men's rights activism" (who's leader literally wants rape to be legalized and was going to hold rallies for it: http://www.washingtontimes.com/…/roosh-v-pro-rape-activist…/ )
I don't know dude, you overanalyze things when film study is one of your hobbies...
textOriginal:

this slasher movie comes out where the entire plot is centered around a counsel of dudes who's goal it is to murder the woman politician who's main platform is "hey, don't kill people".... 
It reminds me of what was happening in 70s slasher films with the spawn of the "final girl" trope and the pattern of brutally murdering half naked women in the middle of another big feminist movement.
It just feels like history is repeating it's self here you know? Because as much as people will say "it's just a movie" movies, especially horror/slasher movies are a reflection of the time they're made. And right now we're living in a time of a modern feminist movement and also an antifeminist movement in the most recognizable form of "men's rights activism" (who's leader literally wants rape to be legalized and was going to hold rallies for it: http://www.washingtontimes.com/…/roosh-v-pro-rape-activist…/ )
I don't know dude, you overanalyze things when film study is one of your hobbies...

textDisplay:
Something I read from a promo for this movie:

_"It's been two years since Leo Barnes stopped himself from a regrettable act of revenge on Purge Night._

Uhhh, hello? Movie promotion writer? Since you clearly didn't watch the last movie, let me explain something to you. THERE ARE NO "REGRETTABLE ACTS OF REVENGE" ON PURGE NIGHT! The entire point of the movie is that you can literally get away with anything on Purge Night,. You can't make a movie about moral choices on the one night of the year when your moral choices don't matter.  This is like saying the Death Star was "regrettably used to blow up a planet." That's what it does. That's ALL it does.
textOriginal:
Something I read from a promo for this movie:

_"It's been two years since Leo Barnes stopped himself from a regrettable act of revenge on Purge Night._

Uhhh, hello? Movie promotion writer? Since you clearly didn't watch the last movie, let me explain something to you. THERE ARE NO "REGRETTABLE ACTS OF REVENGE" ON PURGE NIGHT! The entire point of the movie is that you can literally get away with anything on Purge Night,. You can't make a movie about moral choices on the one night of the year when your moral choices don't matter.  This is like saying the Death Star was "regrettably used to blow up a planet." That's what it does. That's ALL it does.

textDisplay:
Liked the first movie, but I guess the producers actually didn't like the enthusiastic reception it received, since it was SUPPOSED to be a dark satire, but a lot of people didn't take it that way.
The second movie was their way of paying penance for being so politically incorrect...and it was the usual politically correct garbage we've come to expect from Hollywood.
Shame....since it could have been really different and interesting.
 Looks like this is going to continue the downhill spiral; not going to waste my money this time!

textDisplay:
I sometimes wish the Purge movies would explore different aspects of crime than just murder. Not EVERYONE would go out and kill someone, there are people who would see the purge as a opportunity to steal things like the newest ps4 or xbox, movies, food, money etc. 
Yes, murderous psychopaths as shown in these films exist and there would definitely be murders on Purge night but it seems like that's the only aspect of crime that's out there in these films and the longer this series goes on, it will eventually run out of steam and become boring.
There are other thongs people would do on a free night like this. Steal, murder, deal illegal drugs publicly.. other things or just stay at home..
It would be interesting if a purge film had three story arcs on three different sets of people doing different things on Purge night. One story arc would follow a group of (likable) psychopaths going on a murder spree (murder aspect), another story arc have a group of people or teenagers having fun going around the city stealing things (robbery aspect) and another have a family just trying to survive the night at home when a group of angry druggies try to break in and kill them because the husband who secretly has a drug problem has been secretly buying drugs from them and still owes their money. (illegal drugs aspect)

I don't know something different, rather than the same things happening every film: Showing characters who need to survive a world of raving maniacs killing people left and right for no reason. This would bring a breath of fresh air to the franchise if it ever were to continue. But looks like its trying to be the last.. but that's the thing about Hollywood, you never know which one is truly the last film in a successful franchise.

In the best way. The social satire looks great, the violence and atmosphere are positively disturbing... I don't know if these movies are going to be nearly as interesting if we see a substantial change in America's political climate, but for the moment, this is fucking great.

I do hope we see this story come to an end, like we see this senator elected and this is the end of The Purge. Because the thing is, we can keep having these movies and keep the stakes high, as long as they keep changing protagonists, we won't know who will survive if we go back to an earlier Purge night. I just want this one, since it's supposed to be about the end, to be about the end.
textOriginal:
I don't know what the last movie, but holy shit, ESPECIALLY this one are doing so right, but this is legit hard to watch. Like, stomach turning, batshit insanity, disgusting hard to watch. In the best way. The social satire looks great, the violence and atmosphere are positively disturbing... I don't know if these movies are going to be nearly as interesting if we see a substantial change in America's political climate, but for the moment, this is fucking great.

I do hope we see this story come to an end, like we see this senator elected and this is the end of The Purge. Because the thing is, we can keep having these movies and keep the stakes high, as long as they keep changing protagonists, we won't know who will survive if we go back to an earlier Purge night. I just want this one, since it's supposed to be about the end, to be about the end.

textDisplay: Humanity? What's humanity? This is why Donald Trump shouldn't surprise people. Perhaps there is no such thing as humanity. Perhaps deep down we ARE a species of animalistic savages which can never learn to love each other as brothers and sisters, regardless of physical, mental, cultural, and political differences. Perhaps we should have gone gently into that good night a long time ago. But I refuse to believe that. I refuse to believe that this blood and death could ever hold humanity forever. There is no question that it has before, such as in World War II. But so long as a mother or father looks at their child and knows instinctively that they want nothing but the best life for him or her, as he or she has given them the greatest gift life can offer - life itself - I know that there is still goodness in this world, and that someday that love of and for life will be extended into a love of the lives of ALL others. You might say I'm a dreamer, but I'm not the only one.
textOriginal: Humanity? What's humanity? This is why Donald Trump shouldn't surprise people. Perhaps there is no such thing as humanity. Perhaps deep down we ARE a species of animalistic savages which can never learn to love each other as brothers and sisters, regardless of physical, mental, cultural, and political differences. Perhaps we should have gone gently into that good night a long time ago. But I refuse to believe that. I refuse to believe that this blood and death could ever hold humanity forever. There is no question that it has before, such as in World War II. But so long as a mother or father looks at their child and knows instinctively that they want nothing but the best life for him or her, as he or she has given them the greatest gift life can offer - life itself - I know that there is still goodness in this world, and that someday that love of and for life will be extended into a love of the lives of ALL others. You might say I'm a dreamer, but I'm not the only one.

textDisplay:
I would imagine travel and such is cancelled and or barred during the purge. 
The Idea behind the purge is not to release aggressive tendencies but a government planned genocide by the wealthy on the poor, this has been highlighted in every Purge film, the second film showed that majority of the people refuse to purge so the government hires squads to kidnap poor people and or kill them. 

Obviously in the Purge universe, America was taken over by tyrants called the new founding fathers, the key  words are [new founding fathers], meaning that the old United States was either reorganised or disbanded and remade in some major way. We don't know the situation outside the USA, from the purge websites etc, a military draft is in effect and it's possible the USA is at war  or in deep trouble with Europe, the Middle East or all of the above. 
Also being a politician or senator does not automatically make you wealthy. Here in the UK, many MPs are not that wealthy, then again many are.

The second Purge film was much better than the first and the concept is interesting.

Obviously in the Purge universe, America was taken over by tyrants called the new founding fathers, the key  words are [new founding fathers], meaning that the old United States was either reorganised or disbanded and remade in some major way. We don't know the situation outside the USA, from the purge websites etc, a military draft is in effect and it's possible the USA is at war  or in deep trouble with Europe, the Middle East or all of the above. 
Also being a politician or senator does not automatically make you wealthy. Here in the UK, many MPs are not that wealthy, then again many are.

The second Purge film was much better than the first and the concept is interesting.

textDisplay: The Purge series has always been a very riveting and disturbing film series, mainly because the "purge" does exist symbolically in American culture. Yes we don't allow people do go out and legally murder people on a certain day...but we allow them to "murder" in a different way. We murder people with hate, with money, with power and greed and repression, with discrimination...and many of these people are doing these things, individualistically, in the name of the Great American Dream.
textOriginal: The Purge series has always been a very riveting and disturbing film series, mainly because the "purge" does exist symbolically in American culture. Yes we don't allow people do go out and legally murder people on a certain day...but we allow them to "murder" in a different way. We murder people with hate, with money, with power and greed and repression, with discrimination...and many of these people are doing these things, individualistically, in the name of the Great American Dream.

textDisplay: In the first movie we observed a rich family with a good security system like you described, but the second movie focused on poor families who lived in apartments and didn't have the money or access to well-fortified establishments. Putting a bookcase in front of a door wouldn't do much considering the wealthy people purging had military gear and military weapons and battering rams. The movies have the preface that crime goes down and the economy goes up, so that's a reasonable buffer to handle SOME of the damages costs, but you're right: it would cost billions of dollars and even if the economy has gone up, it likely hasn't gone up that much. Tl;dr no one is safe because barricading a door won't do shit and the economy is shit.
textOriginal: In the first movie we observed a rich family with a good security system like you described, but the second movie focused on poor families who lived in apartments and didn't have the money or access to well-fortified establishments. Putting a bookcase in front of a door wouldn't do much considering the wealthy people purging had military gear and military weapons and battering rams. The movies have the preface that crime goes down and the economy goes up, so that's a reasonable buffer to handle SOME of the damages costs, but you're right: it would cost billions of dollars and even if the economy has gone up, it likely hasn't gone up that much. Tl;dr no one is safe because barricading a door won't do shit and the economy is shit.

textDisplay: It's amazing to see how far this series has come since its first entry in 2013. I remember first hearing about the concept and it terrifying me. All of the hype building up to the first film made me very excited, but after seeing the film opening weekend, I was massively let down. Instead of exploring the horrific events that occur in the streets (innocent people being targeted and murdered, revenge stories, etc) the filmmakers chose to put a weird twist on the home invasion genre, seemingly with the purge occurring in the background as a plot device, rather than the purge being the actual PLOT itself. Then, the sequel was announced and my worries were somewhat put to rest as rumor began to spread that the film would explore the effects of the purge in the streets and on normal people. The second film was a well made thriller in my opinion, and was a lot of what I expected out of the first film. Now, with the third entry soon to be released, I'm pleasantly surprised to see the political direction they're choosing to take the series. What started out as a low-budget, high concept film that had grandiose undertones which were never fully explored, has now turned into a series that I can actually say I like and I'm looking forward to. It's interesting to be looking at this series this way, too, because Ouija (another Blumhouse series) seems to be taking the same route. Whereas the first Ouija film was a critical failure but did well at the box-office, Jason Blum seems to actually be paying attention to the QUALITY of his films on top of the money. I say this because he hired Mike Flanagan (Oculus, Hush) to write and direct the Ouija sequel, and Jason has openly said in interviews that he felt the first Ouija film didn't quite hit the mark, but that he believes the sequel will leave people pleasantly surprised. Jason Blum doesn't get the credit he deserves. Sure, it may seem like he's only in the business for the money with his low-budget model and such. But I believe he truly cares about the horror genre as well. Why else would he be listening to the critics and trying to improve on his franchises? Keep up the good work, Blumhouse!
textOriginal: It's amazing to see how far this series has come since its first entry in 2013. I remember first hearing about the concept and it terrifying me. All of the hype building up to the first film made me very excited, but after seeing the film opening weekend, I was massively let down. Instead of exploring the horrific events that occur in the streets (innocent people being targeted and murdered, revenge stories, etc) the filmmakers chose to put a weird twist on the home invasion genre, seemingly with the purge occurring in the background as a plot device, rather than the purge being the actual PLOT itself. Then, the sequel was announced and my worries were somewhat put to rest as rumor began to spread that the film would explore the effects of the purge in the streets and on normal people. The second film was a well made thriller in my opinion, and was a lot of what I expected out of the first film. Now, with the third entry soon to be released, I'm pleasantly surprised to see the political direction they're choosing to take the series. What started out as a low-budget, high concept film that had grandiose undertones which were never fully explored, has now turned into a series that I can actually say I like and I'm looking forward to. It's interesting to be looking at this series this way, too, because Ouija (another Blumhouse series) seems to be taking the same route. Whereas the first Ouija film was a critical failure but did well at the box-office, Jason Blum seems to actually be paying attention to the QUALITY of his films on top of the money. I say this because he hired Mike Flanagan (Oculus, Hush) to write and direct the Ouija sequel, and Jason has openly said in interviews that he felt the first Ouija film didn't quite hit the mark, but that he believes the sequel will leave people pleasantly surprised. Jason Blum doesn't get the credit he deserves. Sure, it may seem like he's only in the business for the money with his low-budget model and such. But I believe he truly cares about the horror genre as well. Why else would he be listening to the critics and trying to improve on his franchises? Keep up the good work, Blumhouse!

textDisplay:
Okay wait just a second. You mean to tell me that the evil dystopian government that has created The Purge as a means to control the population STILL HOLDS DEMOCRATIC ELECTIONS!?

I mean, don't get me wrong. I love The Purge movies, despite how much you need to suspend your disbelief about the statistics surrounding it. But I mean, at least you could always accredit this horrible night to "The New Founding Fathers" being an evil dictatorship wanting an excuse to wipe out the poor people. I thought that was why they had rebels who went around shooting purgers! Because armed rebellion was the only way!

But now you mean to tell me they could've just elected someone who wanted to stop it this entire time!? xD
textOriginal:
Okay wait just a second. You mean to tell me that the evil dystopian government that has created The Purge as a means to control the population STILL HOLDS DEMOCRATIC ELECTIONS!?

I mean, don't get me wrong. I love The Purge movies, despite how much you need to suspend your disbelief about the statistics surrounding it. But I mean, at least you could always accredit this horrible night to "The New Founding Fathers" being an evil dictatorship wanting an excuse to wipe out the poor people. I thought that was why they had rebels who went around shooting purgers! Because armed rebellion was the only way!

But now you mean to tell me they could've just elected someone who wanted to stop it this entire time!? xD

textDisplay: I almost passed on the last one and I'm pretty sure I'll pass on this one. To think that Trump, who I personally do not cheer for, or any politician or Christian on either side of the aisle would condone or sanction the Purge is ridiculous. For a dystopian novel or film to be effective, there must be tie-ins to the real world which may or may not be turned completely on their head by the author or screenwriter. I understand this as well as anyone else. However, this is more than achieved by the excessive flag-waving, the largely skewed view of the American way which comes with the flag-waving, and the propaganda. Thus, there is no need to attack a certain political candidate. Doing so comes across as extremely tacky.
textOriginal: I almost passed on the last one and I'm pretty sure I'll pass on this one. To think that Trump, who I personally do not cheer for, or any politician or Christian on either side of the aisle would condone or sanction the Purge is ridiculous. For a dystopian novel or film to be effective, there must be tie-ins to the real world which may or may not be turned completely on their head by the author or screenwriter. I understand this as well as anyone else. However, this is more than achieved by the excessive flag-waving, the largely skewed view of the American way which comes with the flag-waving, and the propaganda. Thus, there is no need to attack a certain political candidate. Doing so comes across as extremely tacky.

textDisplay: WHAT IS PURGE NIGHT?! LOL
textOriginal: WHAT IS PURGE NIGHT?! LOL

textDisplay: Once a year (March 21) starting at 7:00pm, all crime is legal for 12 hours. That night is called Purge Night. No hospitals, police stations, fire stations, etc. are open until 7:00 am on March 22. Certain people in the government are safe and certain weapons cannot be used. The night is meant to reduce poverty, the amount of jobless people and keep the amount of people in America at a "good" number. But people have stopped killing so in the second movie, it was revealed that people in the government are killing people themselves and paying people to kill for them. This is basically Purge Night. This is actually funny for me because my birthday is March 21 so my birthday is Purge Day. Sorry this is so long. I just wanted to give you an in-depth description.

textDisplay:
Common sense solutions:

1) If you live on the East Coast of the United States, you can drive to Niagara Falls, ON or anywhere in Quebec to avoid the purge. Obviously, just one night would do it. 

2) If you live in the Great Lakes Region, drive to Windsor, Ontario (for those who are unfamiliar, it's on the other side of the Detroit River) and stay there for one night.

3)If you live in the West Coast, you have the choice to drive to Vancouver or Tijuana (depending on whichever's closer) and stay there for the night.

4) If you live in the Southwest, drive to a border town in Mexico and stay there for one night.



Side Note: I'm sorry Hawaiians, there was just no possible way you could have avoided the Purge.

textDisplay: The purge would not be anything like it is displayed in this movie if it were actually a part of society. (I am completely aware of the horrible mass killings of people and how messed up society can be in certain parts of the world). But in the United States I highly doubt that one person or a group of people would go around killing people for what ever reason. Yes there would be murder but not as large of a killing spree as the movie portrays it. Is danger present during the purge, why of course. It is the purge after all.

textDisplay:
Buy a house in Canada for me and my family. I will stay behind for the purge for the year I left and head into the countryside to steal from the stores not many people will go to. Like small town gaming stores and stuff. Some rules
1: No big cities. That is a no-no
2: Don't get in a huge truck unless necessary. That will attract attention that will not be good for your health.
3: Stay away from big stores such as Wal-Mart, Target, Wegmens, ETC. Many people will go there. Some of them are not so nice.
4: NO MALLS 
5: Carry a good sized knife. If you drop your gun while in a fight or you don't want to shoot it in case there are people around you.  
6: Carry 2 guns if you can. Carry a pistol. I suggest a revolver. You can fix a jam easier. Also bring an automatic like an AK 47 or something of that sort. Something that is used or was used by the military and is known for reliability
7: Don't go hunting for people. Walk around a city or where every you are and find them on your way. 
8: If you can STAY HOME!!! Block down your house. Some examples are pit traps in your yard that are full of water and have a mechanism that puts a cover over top of it so that they drown.  Block your windows. Use nails, tape, duck tape, staples, and more nails to hold up sheet metal and wood to stop them from coming in your windows. Place a piece of sheet metal on the outside of the window. If you can pull the shutters in from the sides,  Put wood on your side of the window and place sheet metal over that. Do that for every window. Block all doors with whatever you can. Place the cars in the garage and use them to block the garage. Buy pieces of tree braches. Like big braches and put them in front of the garage. If someone tries to burst through the garage. They would have to get through the tree branches will will be a pain to do.  Get everybody to the top floor and block the staircase from the bottom and top. All stay in one room and arm yourselves. Keep a distance from each other though just in case they change their mind on hiding with the family and try to kill you. 
       That are some rules to surviving the Purge. #8 , #5, #1, #2 and #4 are good for any apocalypse or event like this. If the world goes crazy. Those are good steps to take

textDisplay: Look, this idea is batshit crazy, but it does tickle the imagination.  This would be a very extreme form of population control... and yes, the poor and downtrodden would be hit pretty hard those first few years..  then our 7/11's would go un staffed, the coffee shops would go unstaffed, supermarkets, clothing stores... yeah, society itself would just crumble under the loss of the very foundation that keeps it going.. Sure the middle class would bash it out, hell I would make it a numbers night.. how many can I knock of the list...  but really, after the first two or three years, society would have been hollowed out and nonfunctioning.    I suppose that would solve the question of immigration...  either no one would want to come here or those that did come here would be food for the machine the next year..    fun thought experiment...  This would be an end to a nation.

This is not a test, this is your Emergency Broadcast System. Announcing the commencement of the annual purge sanctioned by the U.S. Government.

Weapons of class four and lower have been authorized for use during the purge. All other weapons are restricted.

 

Government officials of ranking 10 have been granted immunity and shall not be harmed.

Commencing at the siren, any and all crime (including murder) will be legal for 12 continuous hours.

Police, fire, and Emergency Medical services will be unavailable until tomorrow morning at 7:00 a.m. When the purge concludes.

Blessed be our new founding fathers and America... A nation reborn.

May God be with you all."

textDisplay: +zendrasky there's a lot of plot holes and continuity issues. For instance, most crimes aren't permitted so why would it really drop crime rates? Secondly, a country having this type of tradition would shoo away anyone with some common sense. Everyone would likely leave the US other than serial killers and psychopaths. Wouldn't it certainly be cheaper and safer to buy a plane ticket to Canada than to invest in sofisticated security systems and bodyguards? And why would everyone suddenly have the motivation to kill? There are places in the world without law enforcement and people don't go around cutting each other's heads off. I haven't seen any of these movies and I really don't like the modern Horror genre because I think it's just an easy way for studios to get money.
textOriginal: +zendrasky there's a lot of plot holes and continuity issues. For instance, most crimes aren't permitted so why would it really drop crime rates?
